title: Maitri Baagh Zoo
tagline: Maitri Baagh Zoo — wildlife reserve of Chhattisgarh

overview.short: Maitri Bagh Zoo (also known as Garden of Friendship) is a tourist location in Bhilai, India which was established as a symbol of friendship between the Soviet Union and India.
overview.description: Maitri Bagh Zoo (also known as Garden of Friendship) is a tourist location in Bhilai, India which was established as a symbol of friendship between the Soviet Union and India. It is the largest and oldest zoo of Chhattisgarh and previously of the undivided Madhya Pradesh.

howToReach.roadNote: Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

topPlaces.name: Jayanti Stadium
topPlaces.description: The Jayanti Stadium in Bhilai is a multi-purpose stadium at Indra Place in Bhilai, India. The 20,000-capacity stadium is used mostly for football and field hockey and also for athletics.

topPlaces.name: Jama Mosque, Bhilai
topPlaces.description: The Jama Mosque, or Jama Masjid, is a Sunni Islam mosque, located in Bhilai, Chhattisgarh, India. The mosque was completed in the 1960s and can accommodate 3,000 worshippers at a time and is one of the largest mosques in the state, and also in Asia.

topPlaces.name: Mohammad Aeltemesh
topPlaces.description: Mohammad Aeltemesh Rein was an Indian lawyer who practised before the Supreme Court of India from 1978 until the early 2000s. "A lawyer once enrolled under the Advocates Act, may practice throughout India." This contention was raised by him before the Honourable Supreme Court of India upon which the Government was asked to notify section 30 of the Advocates Act.

topPlaces.name: Bhilai Steel Plant
topPlaces.description: The Bhilai Steel Plant (BSP), located in Bhilai, in the Indian state of Chhattisgarh, is India's first and main producer of steel rails, as well as a major producer of wide steel plates and other steel products. The plant also produces steel and markets various chemical by-products from its coke ovens and coal chemical plant.

topPlaces.name: Bhilai
topPlaces.description: Bhilai is a city in Durg district of the Indian state of Chhattisgarh, in eastern central India. Along with its twin-city Durg, the urban agglomeration of Durg-Bhilai Nagar has a population of more than a million, making it the second most populous urban area in Chhattisgarh after Raipur.

topPlaces.name: Hemchand Yadav Vishwavidyalaya
topPlaces.description: Hemchand Yadav Vishwavidyalaya or Hemchand Yadav University, formerly Durg Vishwavidyalaya, is a state university in Durg, Chhattisgarh, India. It was established in 2015 and later renamed in 2018.

topPlaces.name: NSPCL Bhilai Power Plant
topPlaces.description: The NSPCL Bhilai Power Plant is a coal-fired captive power station at Bhilai in Durg district, Chhattisgarh, India.

topPlaces.name: Electric Loco Shed, Bhilai
topPlaces.description: Electric Loco Shed, Bhilai is a motive power depot performing locomotive maintenance and repair facility for electric locomotives of the Indian Railways, located at Bhilai of the South East Central Railway zone in Chhattisgarh, India.

items.note: The Jayanti Stadium in Bhilai is a multi-purpose stadium at Indra Place in Bhilai, India. The 20,000-capacity stadium is used mostly for football and field hockey and also for athletics.

The Jama Mosque, or Jama Masjid, is a Sunni Islam mosque, located in Bhilai, Chhattisgarh, India. The mosque was completed in the 1960s and can accommodate 3,000 worshippers at a time and is one of the largest mosques in the state, and also in Asia.

Mohammad Aeltemesh Rein was an Indian lawyer who practised before the Supreme Court of India from 1978 until the early 2000s. "A lawyer once enrolled under the Advocates Act, may practice throughout India." This contention was raised by him before the Honourable Supreme Court of India upon which the Government was asked to notify section 30 of the Advocates Act.

The Bhilai Steel Plant (BSP), located in Bhilai, in the Indian state of Chhattisgarh, is India's first and main producer of steel rails, as well as a major producer of wide steel plates and other steel products. The plant also produces steel and markets various chemical by-products from its coke ovens and coal chemical plant.

Bhilai is a city in Durg district of the Indian state of Chhattisgarh, in eastern central India. Along with its twin-city Durg, the urban agglomeration of Durg-Bhilai Nagar has a population of more than a million, making it the second most populous urban area in Chhattisgarh after Raipur.
Hemchand Yadav Vishwavidyalaya or Hemchand Yadav University, formerly Durg Vishwavidyalaya, is a state university in Durg, Chhattisgarh, India. It was established in 2015 and later renamed in 2018.

items.note: The NSPCL Bhilai Power Plant is a coal-fired captive power station at Bhilai in Durg district, Chhattisgarh, India.

Electric Loco Shed, Bhilai is a motive power depot performing locomotive maintenance and repair facility for electric locomotives of the Indian Railways, located at Bhilai of the South East Central Railway zone in Chhattisgarh, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Maitri Baagh Zoo?
faq.a: The best time to visit Maitri Baagh Zoo is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Maitri Baagh Zoo?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Swami Vivekananda Airport, Raipur (40 km) and the nearest railway station is Raipur Junction (30 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Maitri Baagh Zoo?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Maitri Baagh Zoo at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Maitri Baagh Zoo?
faq.a: Stays in Maitri Baagh Zoo start from ₹2,500 per night, with options across 5 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Maitri Baagh Zoo?
faq.a: The top attractions are Jayanti Stadium, Jama Mosque, Bhilai, Mohammad Aeltemesh, Bhilai Steel Plant, Bhilai.
